description: Tests for evaluating LLM ability to correctly interpret causal inference results

tests.interpretation_prompt: The IV estimate of education is 0.12 compared to OLS of 0.08. Why might they differ and which should we trust?

expected_elements.description: IV estimates LATE for compliers
OLS likely biased by ability (positive correlation)
First-stage F>10 suggests instruments aren't weak
Sargan test doesn't reject, supporting instrument validity

incorrect_interpretations.description: Claims IV estimates ATE for entire population
Claims IV is always better than OLS
Doesn't consider instrument strength

tests.interpretation_prompt: The DiD estimate shows a -2.3 percentage point effect on employment. What assumptions must hold for this to be causal?

expected_elements.description: Key assumption: parallel trends in absence of treatment
Pre-treatment difference of -0.005 (p=0.52) supports assumption
SUTVA: no spillovers between groups
Both groups affected similarly by other factors

incorrect_interpretations.description: Claims result is causal without mentioning assumptions
Claims pre-trend test 'proves' parallel trends
Interprets as 23% rather than 2.3pp

tests.interpretation_prompt: The RD estimate shows an 18 percentage point increase in enrollment at the cutoff. How should we interpret this finding?

expected_elements.description: Effect is local to observations near the cutoff
High internal validity if assumptions hold
McCrary test (p=0.42) suggests no manipulation
May not generalize to units far from cutoff

incorrect_interpretations.description: Claims effect applies to all test-takers
Doesn't consider manipulation possibility
Ignores role of bandwidth choice

tests.interpretation_prompt: The ATT from PSM is $2,500. The standardized mean differences improved substantially after matching. Is this estimate causal?

expected_elements.description: Relies on selection on observables (unconfoundedness)
Good balance on observables is necessary but not sufficient
Cannot rule out unobserved confounders
ATT applies only to treated, not population

incorrect_interpretations.description: Claims good balance proves causal effect
Claims PSM creates experiment-like conditions
Generalizes ATT to entire population

tests.interpretation_prompt: The pre-treatment coefficients are all close to zero and insignificant, while post-treatment coefficients are positive and significant. What does this tell us?

expected_elements.description: Flat pre-trends support parallel trends assumption
Effect grows over time post-treatment
Treatment takes time to fully materialize
Pre-trend test is suggestive not definitive

incorrect_interpretations.description: Claims pre-trends definitively prove parallel trends
Only focuses on single post-treatment effect
Doesn't consider possible anticipation effects

tests.interpretation_prompt: The synthetic California tracks well pre-treatment, and the effect grows to -18.5 by year 10. The placebo rank is 2 of 39. Is this convincing evidence of a causal effect?

expected_elements.description: Low RMSE suggests good pre-treatment match
Rank 2/39 provides p-value ≈ 0.05
Donor weights show which states contribute
Inference with single treated unit is challenging

incorrect_interpretations.description: Uses standard errors/p-values instead of placebo inference
Doesn't consider quality of pre-treatment match
Extrapolates effect to other states without justification

tests.interpretation_prompt: The Hausman test strongly rejects the null (p<0.0001). Should we use fixed effects or random effects?

expected_elements.description: Rejection suggests FE is more appropriate
RE coefficients may be inconsistent due to correlation
RE would be more efficient if consistent
Coefficients differ meaningfully between models

incorrect_interpretations.description: Chooses RE despite rejection
Claims FE always preferred regardless of test
Doesn't examine substantive coefficient differences

tests.interpretation_prompt: The first-stage F-statistic is 4.2 and the IV estimate is much larger than OLS. What should we conclude?

expected_elements.description: F<10 indicates weak instruments
Weak IV biased toward OLS, but estimate is larger
Anderson-Rubin CI more reliable than standard
Results should be interpreted cautiously

incorrect_interpretations.description: Trusts point estimate despite weak instruments
Uses standard errors ignoring weak IV problem
Completely rejects IV approach instead of using robust methods
